title: Grossularia glandulifera
taxonomy: Grossularia glandulifera is classified within the family Rosaceae, which includes a wide range of flowering plants commonly known as rose family plants. It belongs to the genus Grossularia, which is a subgenus of the genus Ribes. This classification places Grossularia glandulifera in the same family as currants and gooseberries, which are also known for their edible fruits. The family Rosaceae is further divided into various subfamilies, with Grossularia glandulifera being part of the Amygdaloideae subfamily. The classification of Grossularia glandulifera is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the Rosaceae family.

intro: Grossularia glandulifera, also known as Chinese hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding digestion, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Grossularia glandulifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and organic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
